title: valeriana sambucifolia subsp. procurrens
intro: Valeriana sambucifolia subsp. procurrens, also known as a subspecies of the valerian plant, is a perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region characterized by its fragrant flowers and leaves. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include its sedative and anxiolytic effects, attributed to the presence of valerenic acid and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Valeriana sambucifolia subsp. procurrens include improved sleep quality, reduced anxiety and stress levels, and relief from muscle and joint pain. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in the form of teas, tinctures, and essential oils, which are often used to promote relaxation and alleviate symptoms of insomnia and restlessness.
